title: Odkud jste? and Unit 1 review
summary: Ask where people are from, answer with z or ze, and consolidate the main Unit 1 communication patterns.
objectives: Ask where someone is from with odkud
Use common z and ze place-name forms
Understand basic genitive changes in familiar place names
Review introductions, descriptions, possession, age, and plural identification

sections.heading: Asking where someone is from
sections.body: Odkud means from where. Use jste formally or with several people, jsi informally, and je for a third person.

sections.heading: Answering with z or ze
sections.body: Answer with jsem, je, or jsou plus z and a changed place name. These are genitive forms; focus first on common forms relevant to you rather than memorizing every ending.

sections.heading: Common place-name patterns
sections.body: Feminine hard place names often use -y; neuter hard names use -a; some masculine names use -a or -u; soft names commonly use -e or remain -í. Ze helps before difficult consonant clusters and words beginning with s or z.

sections.heading: Unit 1 communication review
sections.body: Combine the core patterns from the unit to introduce yourself, identify and describe people or objects, ask age and ownership, and talk about origin.
examples: To je moje kamarádka. Jmenuje se Eva.

sections.heading: Unit 1 grammar review
sections.body: Check agreement across gender, animacy, and number. Keep identifying to unchanged in To je and To jsou; change demonstratives and adjectives when they directly modify nouns.
examples: ten nový profesor / ta nová profesorka / to nové auto
ti noví profesoři / ty nové knihy / ta nová auta
To je nový profesor. / To jsou noví profesoři.

vocabulary.note: Used to ease pronunciation before some consonant clusters or s/z.

resources.title: Odkud jste? — Where are you from?
resources.note: Origin questions, z/ze, and common place-name forms.

resources.title: Unit 1 table of contents
resources.note: Source sequence and Unit 1 communicative goals.

exercises.prompt: Which question politely asks where someone is from?
exercises.explanation: Odkud jste? means Where are you from? in formal or plural address.

exercises.prompt: Which answer correctly says Sára is from Slovakia?
exercises.explanation: The source form is ze Slovenska.

exercises.prompt: Type the Czech phrase: These are my books.
exercises.explanation: Plural identification uses To jsou; feminine plural possession uses moje.

exercises.prompt: Which phrase correctly means those new cars?
exercises.explanation: Auta are neuter plural: ta nová auta.
